title: Write and Diagnose CSS Rules
summary: Build and verify css rules syntax in survey stylesheet parser investigation through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain css rules syntax from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply css rules syntax without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

steps.title: 04 · Read the survey stylesheet parser investigation case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which claim preserves css rules syntax when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: survey stylesheet parser investigation.
steps.why: Write and Diagnose CSS Rules makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 4 in survey stylesheet parser investigation.

content.text: A CSS rule combines a selector with a declaration block. Each declaration has a property and value. Comments document intent, and at-rules add conditional or structural instructions. Browsers normally discard an invalid declaration or rule and continue parsing later valid input, so DevTools source, parsed rules, warnings, and computed values reveal the actual failure boundary. Apply it to survey stylesheet parser investigation.

Survey stylesheet parser investigation case 4: add semicolons between selectors because selectors are declarations.
